title: Why Aberdeen Group is Focusing on Enterprise IT Integration
author: Peter S. Kastner
subject_domain: Enterprise IT Integration; SOA; EII; infrastructure megatrends
methodology: Internal Aberdeen perspective document; analyst commentary on megatrends
abstract: Aberdeen's Enterprise IT Integration practice perspective explaining the firm's focus area. Kastner outlines six megatrends shaping IT in 2006: programmer productivity stagnation and SOA as the remedy; SOA/Web Services adoption trajectory; deconstruction of processing at core/edge/network; mobility becoming the norm; real-time business intelligence (RTBI); and knowledge worker productivity. Argues for a strategic IT architecture that accumulates synergy across projects, with the practice providing fact-based benchmarking and best-practice research.
importance_rationale: Internal perspective document articulating Aberdeen's practice thesis rather than primary survey data. Useful as a primary-source record of how a leading analyst firm framed enterprise IT integration circa 2006.
relevance_rationale: Directly states Kastner's analytical framework for SOA, EII, and IT integration — foundational context for understanding his other SOA-era studies.
prescience_rationale: Correctly anticipates SOA becoming the majority development paradigm by ~2010, mainstreaming of mobility, rise of real-time BI/analytics, and server virtualization. The 'network is the computer' / edge processing theme predates cloud/edge computing discourse.
